msgs.text:
Turn 42 — cofiltered epimorphic quotient category

I keep the same A2 submission. The attached statement and official PDF require the four-generator format, implicit tame relation, later generators in the 2-core, and profinite exponents submitted modulo M=85667662080; the verifier compares normalized surjection counts.sigma,tau,x0,x1 sigma2 = sigma^40491355905 u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905 u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905 d0 = u0*x0^-1 z0 = x0^sigma2 c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0 x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0 As in Turns 35–41, the proof interprets the exponent as the genuine profinite idempotent ω2∈Z; 40491355905 is only the verifier residue. Turn 41 correctly fixed the semantics: ordinary maps into a finite group H are not the verifier tuples; epimorphisms are.The remaining categorical audit is to prove that the epimorphic quotient system really is a cofiltered finite quotient system of a topologically finitely generated profinite group.

Let F=F (σ,τ,x0,x1). Let Q be the class of finite generated marked quotients ϕ:F↠G such that the distinguished tuple generates G, satisfies the tame relation and the A2 wild relation, and the normal closure of ϕ(x0),ϕ(x1) is a 2-group. In a finite generated target this is equivalent toϕ(x0),ϕ(x1)∈O2(G).SetN=ϕ∈Q⋂ kerϕ,ΓA2=F/N.The key closure point is finite subdirect products. Given finitely many marked quotients ϕi:F↠Gi, let

H=im(F→i∏Gi).Then H is generated by the diagonal distinguished tuple; the tame and wild relations hold coordinatewise; and the normal closure of x0,x1 in H lies inside

H∩i∏O2(Gi),hence is a finite 2-group. Therefore H∈Q, and ker(F→H)=i⋂kerϕi.So finite intersections of marked kernels are again marked kernels. This gives a genuine cofiltered finite quotient system.

It follows that the closed normal subgroupWA2=⟨⟨x0,x1⟩⟩⊂ΓA2 is pro-2: in every cofinal finite quotient from Q, its image is a 2-group.

Now the epimorphic semantics are exact:

If ΓA2 ↠H is an epimorphism, then the image of WA2 is a normal 2-subgroup of H, so x0,x1∈O2 (H), and the tuple is exactly an official verifier tuple.Conversely, any generating verifier tuple in H defines a marked quotient in Q, hence an epimorphism ΓA2 ↠H.Thus

Sur(ΓA2,H)={generating official verifier tuples in H}.This is the precise replacement for the false ordinary-Hom statement. Non-surjective maps still need not satisfy x0,x1∈O2 (H); they are handled, as Turn 41 said, by passing to their image marked subpair. But surjections—the only maps used by the verifier and by the reconstruction lemma—match exactly.With this cofiltered quotient-category audit inserted, the final chain is now:decorated marked theorem⇒∣Sur(ΓA2,H)∣=∣Sur(GQ2,H)∣∀H⇒ΓA2≅GQ2.The last implication is the Turn 39 profinite reconstruction lemma: equality of finite surjection counts for all finite H, plus finite generation and Hopficity, reconstructs the profinite group.

ESTABLISHED: Proved the missing cofiltered-quotient audit. The finite marked quotients are closed under finite subdirect products, so ΓA2 is a genuine finitely generated profinite object with WA2 pro-2. Epimorphisms ΓA2 ↠H are exactly the official verifier tuples in H, while non-surjective maps are correctly handled by image marked subpairs.

GAP: The private 5402-group verifier still has not been executed here. The remaining non-external work is a polished writeup of the decorated marked theorem and the local Arf/deep-unit theorem.

NEXT: Fold this cofiltered epimorphic quotient construction into the final note immediately before the reconstruction lemma, then submit the unchanged A2 residue block to the official verifier.[BREAKTHROUGH]
